new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage produced exclusively at a single distillery by distilling a fermented mash made solely from malted barley, using pot stills, classified under CN 2208 30 30.
A distillation process carried out in traditional pot stills (batch distillation vessels), as opposed to continuous column stills, used in the production of single malt whisky.
A fermented mash produced solely from malted barley, used as the feedstock for single malt whisky distillation.

new_classes.label_de: Qualitätsmerkmal: Herkunft aus einer einzelnen Brennerei
new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a spirit indicating that it was produced entirely at a single distillery, as required for single malt whisky classification.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms capture the four key distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) production via pot still distillation (PotStillDistillationProcess), (2) use of a fermented mash made solely from malted barley (MaltedBarleyMash), (3) origin from a single distillery (SingleDistilleryOriginQuality), and (4) membership in the SpiritousBeverage class. A minor gap remains: the axioms do not formally enforce the exclusivity constraint ('only malted barley' — an owl:allValuesFrom restriction on feedstock) because the MaltedBarleyMash class already implies the 'solely malted barley' constraint by definition, but this is expressed via natural-language definition rather than a formal OWL restriction. Additionally, the Scotch whisky geographic origin (Scotland) is not captured as a formal quality, since no ScotlandOriginQuality class exists in the TBox and introducing one would require further geographic modelling. The producedBy property is used to link SingleMaltWhisky to PotStillDistillationProcess following the TBox pattern.
